rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens
Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose
Corporate infra never a forcing function
Host-skill optional; default cohort no host-skill
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block uses 'Push-back moves (trainer delivers in cohort; Nerd delivers only in self-study)' — correct shape

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats prefer in-chat over file-write
No conversations in body — body teaches work, trainer manages room
No reading-burden verification — Claude audits Claude
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'Read what Claude proposes' / 'Read it' are emotional-payoff reads (does it fit your codebase, does it sound like an engineer-to-engineer voice), not correctness verification — allowed per rule 30 carve-out
Plug points name kind of org context (sponsor-stated ADR home, STRIDE skill location, Ex1 surface-map path); body integrates: Phase 3 reads ADR home; Phase 1 reads Ex1 map

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes
rules_evaluated.evidence: Three observable, falsifiable outcomes named (incident-story-precedes-pick / rejects-alternatives-explicitly / four-section ADR at sponsor path)
Three phases, each with dominant failure mode + escape hatch in dedicated maintainer-block table

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing doesn't preview the prompt's payoff
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'What you do:' names activity (invoke skill, pick one, write ADR) + constraint (in your repo's convention), not mechanism or felt-evidence

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Engineer-audience audit fixes default to body callout
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body callouts ('push back if the Decision section reads like compliance', STRIDE-doesn't-fit recovery in Phase 3) are engineer-recoverable; forcing functions live in fenced prompts

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Multi-session ships as packaging, not new primitive
rules_evaluated.evidence: Window callout '*m3-security* (continuing the main lane; m3-quality stays parked)' is single-line packaging, not a taught beat
